title: Bracca
content:
{{Top}} {{Expand|all sections}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:BraccaPlanet-JFO.jpg]] |name=Bracca |region=[[Mid Rim Territories]]''[[Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order]]'' |sector=[[Lantillian sector]]''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia: The Comprehensive Guide to the Star Wars Galaxy]]'' |system=[[Bracca system]] |coordinates=[[Standard Galactic Grid|P-9]][[Star Wars Galaxy Map (poster)|''Star Wars'' Galaxy Map poster]] |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons=1 |routes=[[Randon Run]]{{GalaxyMapHyperlanes|route=Randon Run|canon={{BuildFalconCite|43|Guide to the Galaxy}}|leg=''[[The Essential Atlas]]''}} |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class=Terrestrial |diameter= |atmosphere=[[Atmosphere|Type 1]] |climate=[[Temperature|Temperate]]{{Databank|bracca|Bracca}} |gravity= |terrain=*Junkyards *Plains |water= |interest=*[[Shipbreaking Yard]] *[[Bracca spaceport]]s *[[Eren's place]]{{YJA|Journey to the Bracca Badlands}} *[[Guild store]] *[[Worker Rail Route 213]] *[[Platform thirty-three]]''[[Resistance Reborn]]'' *[[Platform thirty-two]] *[[Platform twenty]] *[[Retired Control Tower]] *[[Bracca Badlands]]{{YJA|Journey to the Bracca Badlands}} |flora= |fauna=*[[Bisnu]]{{YJA|Scrapping for a Song}} *[[Bird]]s{{TBB|Battle Scars}} *[[Ibdis Maw]] *[[Mygora]] *[[Scrap rat]] |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies=*[[Abednedo]] *[[Dianoga]] *[[Human]] *[[Mirialan]] *[[Pantoran]] *[[Pikpik]]{{YJA|Finders Keepers}} *[[Rodian]] |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports=*Decommissioned and destroyed [[starship]]s *Scrap *Ship parts |exports=*Starships *Scrap |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic]] *[[Scrapper Guild]] *[[Galactic Empire]] *[[First Order]] }} {{Quote|It's kind of pretty from up here.''"
"''Sure, if you like depressing industrial space junk.|[[Zay Versio]] and Shriv Suurgav|}} '''Bracca''' was an inhospitable junkyard [[planet]] located in [[the galaxy]]'s [[Mid Rim Territories|Mid Rim]]. Located close to various [[hyperspace route]]s, Bracca's strategic importance made it the site of a [[Bracca Invasion|campaign]] during the [[Clone Wars]]. During the [[Imperial Era|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire]], the [[Scrapper Guild]] operated on the planet, breaking down [[starship]] wreckage and selling the components to the Empire. ==Description== [[File:Bracca_terrain.png|thumb|left|250px|The shipbreaking yard on Bracca]] Bracca was a poor [[Mid Rim Territories|Mid Rim]] [[Planet|world]] dominated by rocky plains. Large swathes of the planet's surface were comprised of [[Salvage yard|junkyards]], with countless decommissioned starships and other machinery spread haphazardly, awaiting destruction. It was orbited by one moon. Given the generally inhospitable nature of the world, the majority of its population was comprised of [[scrapper]]s, who made their living breaking down and selling the trash left on the world. The Scrapper Guild used to fix and build starships, hiring many [[engineer]]s, but by the [[14 BBY|fifth year]] of the Imperial Era, engineers had turned into scrappers. Nearly all services on Bracca were operated by the [[Scrapper Guild]]. Credits earned in a workday were quickly recouped by the Guild due to the prices for food, lodging, and other necessities.''[[The Art of Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order]]'' The scrappers often tipped platforms full of junk into the [[Ibdis Maw]] and harvested the waste products for profit. Via the [[Randon Run]], Bracca was [[Hyperspace route|connected]] to [[Lantillies]], on the [[Perlemian Trade Route]], and [[Uyter]]. The planet's spaceports exported supplies from the scrapyards to the [[Core Worlds]]; these supplies were delivered by an extensive system of high-speed transport trains criss-crossing Bracca's surface. The average shipment would pass through twenty-five checkpoints before reaching its final destination. ===Fauna=== Bracca was home to the superorganism [[Ibdis Maw]] that fed on wreckage from picked over [[Starship|ships]]. The planet also hosted [[mygora]]s, [[bisnu]]s, and [[scrap rat]]s. Mygoras were large creatures that lived in the junkyards of Bracca, using pieces of scrap as body [[armor]]. Bisnus were smaller, but also lived in the junkyards; they were an [[Reproduction|egg]]-laying quadruped with [[Color|purple]] skin and gray [[Hair|fur]]. Scrap rats were a species of [[rat]] that could be found creating nests out of wires and often traveled through the pipes of the planet's wrecked ships. ==History== ===High Republic Era=== Throughout the High Republic Era, Bracca was predominantly operated by independent scrappers, who worked under a "finders-keepers" dynamic. Sometime between [[233 BBY]] and [[232 BBY]],{{YJADate|123B}} Bracca was visited by [[Kai Brightstar]], [[Lys Solay]], [[Nubs]], and [[Nash Durango]] in order to spend time with [[Eren Kitt]] for the day. While there, Kitt's droid, [[M1-M1]], required a fresh [[power cell]]. Upon discovering a crate of power cells, a few [[Pikpik]]s stole the crate, violating the mutual "finders keepers" agreement between scrappers. This event would lead towards greater cooperation between Scrappers, and begin putting an end to the competitive "finders keepers" system altogether. Brightstar, Solay, and Nubs returned, this time to give Kitt some scrap they had scavenged from [[Marlaa Jinara's junkyard]]. When they arrived, they learned that [[Tippo]] and other [[Pikpik]]s had gotten lost in the [[Bracca Badlands|badlands]]. Together they journeyed to find Tippo, who had been captured by two [[Nihil]] [[pirate]]s and forced into working for them. Brightstar, Solay, Kitt and Nubs attempted to rescue the pikpik but during the ensuing firefight a [[Giant dianoga]] was alerted, and the pirates escaped with the pikpik. Tippo and the [[Jedi]] worked together to distract the pirates with the diagona, and saved the pikpik. [[Jedi]] [[Jedi Initiate|younglings]] Brightstar, Solay, and Nubs alongside [[Technician|mechanic]] Nash Durango returned again, sometime between 232 [['ABY-BBY' dating system|BBY]] and [[230 BBY]],{{YJADate|304A}} to find Nash's [[Gender|mom]], [[Kryys Durango]], a [[vio-harp]] for her [[birthday]]. Bracca was Nash's last hope to find the [[harp]], as it was extremely rare. The group looked among the planet's vast junkyards to find it, but when they did, a [[Unidentified scrapper (vio-harp)|scrapper]] took it instead. Disappointed, Nash chased after the scrapper's [[landspeeder]], and the rest of the group went looking for other instruments as a contingency plan. All three members successfully found a [[music]]al instrument, and Nash succeeded in getting back the vio-harp. However, Nash accidentally dropped the harp onto a [[sleep]]ing mygora. She''[[Star Wars: The High Republic Character Encyclopedia]]'' quickly went after it but woke up the creature instead, causing the mygora to attack her. Because of the mygora's defensive nature over the scrap they have collected, the creature would not let Nash go without her returning the harp. Eventually, Nash gave the harp back to the creature, allowing her to escape. ===Clone Wars=== A sudden [[strike on Bracca]] was carried out by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Standard week|weeks]] into the [[Clone Wars]]. The [[Galactic Republic]] sent a [[Cyruss Okent's clone battalion|clone battalion]] led by [[Anakin Skywalker's Knighting Ceremony|newly-promoted]] [[Jedi Knight]] [[Cyruss Okent]] to respond to the attack.[[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']] During the final days of the Clone Wars in [[19 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' the Confederacy attempted to take control of Bracca from the Galactic Republic with the help of their [[Scrapper Guild]] sympathizers in the so-called [[Bracca Invasion]].''[[The Star Wars Book]]'' The [[13th Battalion]], led by [[Jedi General]] [[Jaro Tapal]] and his Padawan [[Cal Kestis]], was sent to secure the world and were successful in their mission. Tapal and Kestis were stationed above Bracca in their [[Venator-class Star Destroyer|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Albedo Brave]]'', but following [[Order 66]], Tapal [[Skirmish aboard the Albedo Brave|was killed]] and Kestis crash-landed on Bracca. ===Imperial Era=== {{Quote|I think it's time someone came forward. I, uh..., I-I've been working on this heap a long time. Way before the war. We refit and rebuild ships. Best in the galaxy. Then came the Empire. And engineers...became scrappers. The workers - just started getting worked. But we all know the truth. We're just...afraid to say it. To the Empire...we're all just expendable.|Prauf|}} [[File:Bracca.png|thumb|right|290px|The ''Stinger Mantis'' flies above Bracca]] Shortly after the [[Proclamation of the New Order|formation]] of the [[Galactic Empire]] in 19 [['ABY-BBY' dating system|BBY]],''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]'' places the events of [[The Bad Batch Season 1|''The Bad Batch'' Season 1]] in [[19 BBY]]. the fugitive [[clone trooper]]s [[Captain]] [[Rex]] and [[Clone Force 99]] traveled to Bracca in order to utilize the medical amenities of a [[Unidentified Venator-class Star Destroyer (Clone Force 99)|Jedi cruiser]] to remove the so-called "Bad Batch's" [[Behavioral modification biochip|inhibitor chips]], as the devices had been used to force the [[Cloning|clone]] [[army]] to turn on the [[Jedi]] and serve the Empire. In the aftermath, the location of the so-called "Bad Batch" was leaked to the Empire by a Scrapper Guild patrol, leading to a [[Mission on Bracca|skirmish]] between them and Imperial forces, and the kidnapping of the young clone [[Omega]] during the battle by notorious [[bounty hunter]] [[Cad Bane]].{{TBB|Reunion}} Five years later, Kestis was still on Bracca awaiting word from the [[Jedi Order]] and was working for the [[Scrapper Guild]], which was for many the only source of [[Galactic Credit Standard|credits]], where it wasn't uncommon for workers to fall to their death whilst scrapping. After a workplace accident forced Kestis to use [[the Force]] to save his co-worker and friend [[Prauf]], the [[Inquisitorius]] was alerted to his presence. Arriving on Bracca, the [[Second Sister]] and [[Ninth Sister]] questioned several of the Scrappers for the whereabouts of the Jedi. When Prauf stood up to them, he was [[Death penalty|executed]] by the Second Sister, forcing Cal to [[Escape from Bracca|flee]] with the help of former Jedi Knight [[Cere Junda]] and [[Latero]] pilot [[Greez Dritus]]. ===Rise of the First Order=== {{Quote|Even if we get to Coronet City and Poe and Charth and whoever are able to lift a few First Order ships, it's not enough.''"
"''I agree. That's why I want you to go to Bracca. Put a team together. Take whoever you need and go get as many starfighters as you can.|Shriv Surgav and Leia Organa|}} Decades later, as [[First Order-Resistance War|war]] sparked up and the [[New Republic]] was [[Hosnian Cataclysm|destroyed]], the [[First Order]] started sending claimed Republic starships for decommissioning at Bracca. General [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]] ordered a team there led by [[Shriv Suurgav]] to [[Mission to Bracca|recover]] starships. The team, codenamed [[Dross Squadron]], infiltrated the Scrapper Guild, but were confronted by [[First Order stormtrooper|stormtroopers]]. With the help of locals, they stole several [[X-wing starfighter]]s, but at the loss of one of their pilots, [[Sanrec Stronghammer]]. ==Inhabitants== The Scrapper Guild was found on the planet, employing many beings in their junkyard business. Members of their workforce belonged to a variety of [[species]], including [[human]]s and [[Abednedo]]s. Many [[dianoga]]s lived among the wreckage of the planet, especially in the ponds and lakes of the Badlands. At least [[Unidentified Dianoga (Bracca)|one dianoga]] lived inhabited the murky waters that sat beneath the scrap of a decommissioned ''Venator''-class Star Destroyer. ==Behind the scenes== Bracca was created for the [[2019]] video game ''[[Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order]]'', developed by [[Respawn Entertainment]]. Prior to the game's release, it appeared in the novel ''[[Resistance Reborn]]'', written by [[Rebecca Roanhorse]] and published in 2019. The design of Bracca was intended to briefly illustrate where ''Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order'' sat on the ''[[Star Wars]]'' timeline. The designing process was "very collaborative," according to Environment Artist [[Evan Cwiertny]].{{ArtStation|username=evancwiertny|name=[[Evan Cwiertny]]|url=artwork/oO51xW|text= Star Wars Jedi:
